mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 5% indicates tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE declines below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in housing starts (HOUST) and real retail sales (RSAFS) signals expanding consumer demand that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with combined HOUST/RSAFS growth in 3+ consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105.5 signals strengthening manufacturing activity that boosts corporate margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows negative 20-day returns in 50%+ of instances where INDPRO exceeds 105.5.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 238 signals inflationary pressures that prompt central bank rate hikes, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 238.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP contraction (GDPC1 < 0) indicates economic recession risks that drive equity market sell-offs, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX shows positive 60-day returns in 50%+ of instances where GDPC1 declines.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain (PAYEMS) below 50k signals weak labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending confidence, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS falls below 50k.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and GDP (GDPC1) signal economic contraction risks that drive equity market declines, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show positive correlation with combined INDPRO/GDPC1 declines in 3+ consecutive quarters.
